tools.name: computer_use_click
tools.description: Click on a UI element by its [ID] from the accessibility tree, or at raw screen coordinates as fallback. Supports single click, double-click, and right-click via the click_type parameter.

element_id.description: The [ID] number of the element from the accessibility tree (preferred)

x.description: X coordinate on screen (fallback when no element_id)

y.description: Y coordinate on screen (fallback when no element_id)

reasoning.description: Explanation of what you see and why you are clicking here

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: computer_use_type_text
tools.description: Type text at the current cursor position. The target field must already be focused (click it first).

reasoning.description: Explanation of what you are typing and why

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: computer_use_key
tools.description: Press a key or keyboard shortcut. Supported: enter, tab, escape, backspace, delete, up, down, left, right, space, cmd+a, cmd+c, cmd+v, cmd+z, cmd+tab, cmd+w, shift+tab, option+tab

key.description: Key or shortcut to press (e.g. enter, tab, cmd+c, cmd+v)

reasoning.description: Explanation of why you are pressing this key

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: computer_use_scroll
tools.description: Scroll within an element by its [ID], or at raw screen coordinates as fallback.

element_id.description: The [ID] number of the element to scroll within (preferred)

x.description: X coordinate on screen (fallback when no element_id)

y.description: Y coordinate on screen (fallback when no element_id)

reasoning.description: Explanation of why you are scrolling

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: computer_use_drag
tools.description: Drag from one element or position to another. Use for moving files, resizing windows, rearranging items, or adjusting sliders.

element_id.description: The [ID] of the source element to drag from (preferred)

x.description: Source X coordinate (fallback when no element_id)

y.description: Source Y coordinate (fallback when no element_id)

to_element_id.description: The [ID] of the destination element to drag to (preferred)

to_x.description: Destination X coordinate (fallback when no to_element_id)

to_y.description: Destination Y coordinate (fallback when no to_element_id)

reasoning.description: Explanation of what you are dragging and why

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: computer_use_wait
tools.description: Wait for the UI to update

reasoning.description: Explanation of what you are waiting for

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: computer_use_open_app
tools.description: Open or switch to a macOS application by name. Preferred over cmd+tab for switching apps — more reliable and explicit.

app_name.description: The name of the application to open (e.g. "Slack", "Safari", "Google Chrome", "VS Code")

reasoning.description: Explanation of why you need to open or switch to this app

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: computer_use_run_applescript
tools.description: Execute an AppleScript to control applications via Apple's scripting bridge. Use this for operations that are more reliable through scripting than UI interaction: setting a browser URL directly, navigating Finder to a path, querying app state (tab count, window titles, document status), or clicking deeply nested menu items. The script's return value (if any) will be reported back. NEVER use "do shell script" — it is blocked for security. Keep scripts short and targeted to a single operation.

script.description: The AppleScript source code to execute

reasoning.description: Explanation of what this script does and why AppleScript is better than UI interaction for this step

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

summary.description: Human-readable summary of what was accomplished

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: computer_use_respond
tools.description: Respond directly to the user with a text answer. Use this when the user is asking a question (about their schedule, meetings, calendar, etc.) rather than asking you to control the computer.

answer.description: The text answer to display to the user

reasoning.description: Explanation of how you determined the answer

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called
